commands.title: Show ARP Table
commands.description: Displays the IPv4 ARP (Address Resolution Protocol) cache, mapping IP addresses to MAC addresses.
commands.sample_output:
Protocol  Address          Age (min)  Hardware Addr   Type   Interface
Internet  10.0.0.1                -   0011.2233.4455  ARPA   GigabitEthernet0/1
Internet  10.0.0.2               12   0011.2233.4466  ARPA   GigabitEthernet0/1
commands.notes: On IOS-XR the keyword 'ip' is dropped: 'show arp'. Age '-' means the entry is for the local interface.

commands.title: Show IP Interface Brief
commands.description: Provides a condensed summary of IP address, interface status and protocol status for all interfaces.
commands.sample_output:
Interface              IP-Address      OK? Method Status                Protocol
GigabitEthernet0/0     10.1.1.1        YES manual up                    up
GigabitEthernet0/1     unassigned      YES unset  administratively down down
commands.notes: IOS-XR uses 'show ipv4 interface brief' instead of 'show ip interface brief'.

commands.title: Show Interfaces (detailed)
commands.description: Displays detailed statistics for interfaces: line/protocol state, MTU, bandwidth, duplex, drops, errors, input/output rate.
commands.sample_output:
GigabitEthernet0/1 is up, line protocol is up
  Hardware is iGbE, address is 0011.2233.4477
  MTU 1500 bytes, BW 1000000 Kbit/sec, DLY 10 usec
  5 minute input rate 1200 bits/sec, 2 packets/sec
commands.notes: NX-OS uses the singular form 'show interface' (also accepts 'show interfaces').

commands.title: Show Interfaces Status
commands.description: Compact table of switch port status, VLAN, duplex, speed and type — very useful for a fast overview.
commands.sample_output:
Port      Name               Status       Vlan       Duplex  Speed Type
Gi1/0/1                      connected    10         a-full  a-1000 10/100/1000BaseTX
commands.notes: This is a switching (Catalyst / Nexus) command; not present on IOS-XR routers.

commands.title: Clear ARP Cache
commands.description: Clears dynamic entries from the ARP cache, forcing re-resolution.
commands.sample_output: (no output on success)
commands.notes: Use with caution on production routers with many hosts — it causes a burst of ARP requests.

commands.title: Show IPv6 Interface Brief
commands.description: Summarizes IPv6 addressing and interface state.
commands.sample_output:
GigabitEthernet0/1    [up/up]
    fe80::211:22ff:fe33:4477
    2001:db8::1
commands.notes: Identical syntax across all four platforms.

commands.title: Show IPv6 Neighbors
commands.description: Displays the IPv6 Neighbor Discovery cache — the IPv6 equivalent of the ARP table.
commands.sample_output:
IPv6 Address                     Age  Link-layer Addr  State  Interface
2001:db8::2                      0    0011.2233.4488   REACH  Gi0/1
commands.notes: NX-OS uses singular 'show ipv6 neighbor'.

commands.title: Show IPv6 Route Table
commands.description: Displays the IPv6 routing table.
commands.notes: IOS-XR inverts the keyword order: 'show route ipv6' rather than 'show ipv6 route'.

commands.title: Show IP Routing Table
commands.description: Displays the IPv4 RIB — best paths installed for forwarding, with the owning protocol and administrative distance/metric.
commands.notes: IOS-XR shortens the command to 'show route'.

commands.title: Show IP Route Summary
commands.description: Shows a per-protocol count of routes in the RIB — quick way to gauge table size and protocol contribution.
commands.sample_output:
Route Source    Networks    Subnets
connected       0           4
bgp 65001       0           184320
commands.notes: Useful before/after a maintenance window to confirm route counts match expectations.

commands.title: Show IP Routing Protocols
commands.description: Lists all IP routing protocols currently running along with their timers, filters and networks advertised.
commands.sample_output:
Routing Protocol is "bgp 65001"
  Sending updates every 0 seconds
commands.notes: Handy first command to run when you inherit an unfamiliar router.

commands.title: Show IP CEF Table
commands.description: Displays the Cisco Express Forwarding table — the actual hardware/software forwarding information base used for packet switching.
commands.sample_output:
10.0.0.0/24
  nexthop 192.0.2.1 GigabitEthernet0/1
commands.notes: On IOS-XR the equivalent is simply 'show cef'.

commands.title: Configure a Static Route
commands.description: Creates a manually configured static route in the RIB.
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show ip route static')
commands.notes: IOS-XR requires entering the 'router static' configuration submode first; NX-OS uses CIDR notation directly on the ip route line.
commands.configuration_mode: Yes (global configuration mode / router static submode on XR)

commands.title: Show BGP Summary
commands.description: Displays the state of all BGP neighbors: uptime, prefixes received, and session state (Idle/Active/Established).
commands.sample_output:
Neighbor        V   AS  MsgRcvd MsgSent   TblVer  InQ OutQ Up/Down  State/PfxRcd
192.0.2.1       4  65001  10234   10200     5501    0    0 3d02h        184320
commands.notes: On IOS-XR use 'show bgp summary' (address-family aware — add 'show bgp ipv4 unicast summary' for explicit AFI/SAFI).

commands.title: Show BGP Neighbor Detail
commands.description: Detailed BGP neighbor session information: capabilities negotiated, hold/keepalive timers, message statistics, address-families.
commands.sample_output:
BGP neighbor is 192.0.2.1, remote AS 65001, external link
  BGP state = Established, up for 3d02h
commands.notes: Very useful for troubleshooting flapping or stuck-in-Active sessions.

commands.title: Show BGP Table
commands.description: Displays the BGP table (Adj-RIB / Loc-RIB) with all learned paths, AS-path, next-hop and best-path indication.
commands.notes: Add 'longer-prefixes' to see all more-specifics of a given supernet.

commands.title: Configure a BGP Neighbor
commands.description: Establishes a new eBGP or iBGP peering relationship.
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show bgp summary')
commands.notes: On NX-OS/IOS-XR you must also enter the address-family (e.g. 'address-family ipv4 unicast') under the neighbor before it exchanges routes.

commands.title: Apply Route-map to BGP Neighbor
commands.description: Applies inbound or outbound routing policy (filtering/manipulation) to a BGP neighbor.
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show route-map' and 'show ip bgp neighbors <ip> routes')
commands.notes: IOS-XR uses RPL (Routing Policy Language, 'route-policy') instead of classic route-maps.

commands.title: Clear/Reset BGP Session
commands.description: Refreshes BGP routing information with a peer without tearing down the TCP session (soft reset), or forces a hard reset if 'soft' is omitted.
commands.sample_output: (no output on success)
commands.notes: Prefer soft reset in production; a hard clear tears down the session and can cause a brief black-hole.

commands.title: Show OSPF Neighbors
commands.description: Displays OSPF adjacency state with each neighbor (Down/Init/2-Way/Full etc.).
commands.sample_output:
Neighbor ID     Pri   State           Dead Time   Address         Interface
10.0.0.2          1   FULL/BDR        00:00:38    10.0.0.2        Gi0/1
commands.notes: A neighbor stuck in 2-Way on a broadcast segment is normal for DROTHER routers; stuck in Exstart usually indicates an MTU mismatch.

commands.title: Show OSPF Database
commands.description: Displays the OSPF Link-State Database (LSDB) contents by LSA type.
commands.sample_output:
OSPF Router with ID (10.0.0.1) (Process ID 1)

 Router Link States (Area 0)
commands.notes: Use to confirm all routers in an area have an identical LSDB (a common cause of routing loops is a torn/desynced LSDB).

commands.title: Show OSPF Interface
commands.description: Shows per-interface OSPF parameters: area, cost, network type, timers, DR/BDR.
commands.sample_output:
GigabitEthernet0/1 is up, line protocol is up
  Internet address 10.0.0.1/24, Area 0
commands.notes: Check that Hello/Dead timers match on both sides of a link if a neighbor won't form.

commands.title: Configure OSPF Process
commands.description: Enables the OSPF process and assigns interfaces/networks to an area.
commands.nx_os:
feature ospf
router ospf <tag>
interface <name>
 ip router ospf <tag> area <area-id>
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show ip ospf neighbor')
commands.notes: NX-OS requires 'feature ospf' first, and assigns interfaces to OSPF directly under the interface config rather than with a 'network' statement.

commands.title: Show ISIS Neighbors
commands.description: Displays IS-IS adjacency state (level, state, holdtime) with neighboring routers.
commands.sample_output:
System Id      Type Interface     IP Address      State Holdtime CircId
R2               L1  Gi0/1         10.0.0.2         UP    24      02
commands.notes: Classic IOS uses the legacy CLNS-based command 'show clns neighbors'; IOS-XE/XR use 'show isis neighbors'. NX-OS uses 'show isis adjacency'.

commands.title: Show ISIS Database
commands.description: Displays the IS-IS Link State Database (LSPs received from all routers in the area/level).
commands.sample_output:
IS-IS Level-1 Link State Database
LSPID     LSP Seq Num  LSP Checksum  LSP Holdtime
commands.notes: Consistent syntax across all four platforms.

commands.title: Show MPLS Interfaces
commands.description: Lists interfaces that have MPLS (LDP/TE) enabled and their operational status.
commands.sample_output:
Interface              IP            Tunnel   BGP Static Operational
GigabitEthernet0/1     Yes(ldp)      No       No  No        Yes
commands.notes: Same syntax on all platforms.

commands.title: Show MPLS Forwarding Table
commands.description: Displays the Label Forwarding Information Base (LFIB) — incoming/outgoing labels and next hop per prefix.
commands.sample_output:
Local  Outgoing    Prefix           Bytes Label   Outgoing   Next Hop
Label  Label       or Tunnel Id     Switched      interface
16     Pop Label   10.0.0.0/24      1234          Gi0/1      192.0.2.1
commands.notes: IOS-XR shortens this to 'show mpls forwarding'.

commands.title: Show MPLS LDP Neighbors
commands.description: Shows the state of LDP sessions with directly/indirectly connected LDP peers.
commands.sample_output:
Peer LDP Ident: 10.0.0.2:0; Local LDP Ident 10.0.0.1:0
    TCP connection: 10.0.0.2.646 - 10.0.0.1.11021
    State: Oper; Msgs sent/rcvd: 120/118
commands.notes: Same syntax across platforms.

commands.title: Show MPLS LDP Bindings
commands.description: Displays the local and remote label bindings learned/advertised via LDP for each prefix.
commands.sample_output:
10.0.0.0/24, rev 4
        local binding:  label: 16
        remote binding: lsr: 10.0.0.2:0, label: 22
commands.notes: Compare local vs remote binding to troubleshoot label mismatches.

commands.title: Show RSVP Neighbors
commands.description: Displays RSVP-TE neighbor adjacency state, used by MPLS Traffic Engineering.
commands.sample_output:
Neighbor         RSVP  Encapsulation
10.0.0.2         Y     ip
commands.notes: On IOS-XR drop the 'ip' keyword: 'show rsvp neighbor'.

commands.title: Show MPLS Traffic Engineering Tunnels
commands.description: Displays state, bandwidth and path of RSVP-signaled MPLS-TE tunnels.
commands.sample_output:
Signalling Summary:
    LSP Tunnels Process:              Running
    Tunnel0    10.0.0.1   10.0.0.5   up     up     0        10:15:23
commands.notes: MPLS-TE is not supported in the same form on NX-OS (data center platform).

commands.title: Show Segment Routing (ISIS) Info
commands.description: Displays Segment Routing (SR-MPLS) SID-to-label bindings learned via IS-IS.
commands.sample_output:
Label   Prefix           Level
16001   10.0.0.1/32      L1
commands.notes: Segment Routing display commands are only present on IOS-XE/XR platforms that support SR-MPLS; not on classic IOS.

commands.title: Show Segment Routing Traffic Engineering Policy
commands.description: Displays configured SR-TE policies, their candidate paths and the resulting SID list programmed into forwarding.
commands.sample_output:
Name: POLICY1  Status: Admin: up  Operational: up
  Candidate-paths:
    Preference: 100
commands.notes: SR-TE is an IOS-XE/IOS-XR feature not present on classic IOS or NX-OS.

commands.title: Show EVPN Summary
commands.description: Shows BGP EVPN address-family neighbor summary — used for EVPN-VXLAN control plane troubleshooting.
commands.sample_output:
Neighbor        V   AS MsgRcvd MsgSent   TblVer  InQ OutQ Up/Down State/PfxRcd
10.0.0.2        4 65001    5023    5010     102    0    0 2d04h        50
commands.notes: Requires 'feature bgp' and 'feature nv overlay' on NX-OS, or the l2vpn evpn address-family activated on IOS-XE/XR.

commands.title: Show EVPN MAC-IP Table
commands.description: Displays MAC and MAC-IP routes learned/advertised via EVPN Type-2 routes.
commands.sample_output:
Topo ID  Mac Address    Prod   Flags        Seq No  Next-Hops
30       0011.2233.44aa L      Local       0       N/A
commands.notes: Command name differs meaningfully per vendor OS in EVPN — always confirm against the exact software train, since EVPN CLI evolves quickly.

commands.title: Show VXLAN VNI/Interfaces
commands.description: Displays configured VXLAN Network Identifiers (VNIs) and their associated NVE interface state.
commands.sample_output:
Interface VNI      Multicast-group VNI State  Type Mode BD    cfg
nve1      10001    n/a             Up     L2   CP   10    CLI
commands.notes: Requires 'feature nv overlay' and 'feature vn-segment-vlan-based' on NX-OS.

commands.title: Show VXLAN NVE Peers
commands.description: Shows remote VTEP (VXLAN Tunnel Endpoint) peers and their reachability/state.
commands.sample_output:
Interface Peer-IP        State LearnType Uptime   Router-Mac
nve1      10.0.0.2       Up    CP        02:15:33 n/a
commands.notes: Peer state 'Up' confirms the underlay reachability + control-plane peering is healthy.

commands.title: Show Policy-Map (QoS)
commands.description: Displays QoS policy-map statistics applied to an interface: matched classes, queue drops, byte/packet counters.
commands.sample_output:
Service-policy output: QOS-OUT
  Class-map: VOICE (match-any)
    10 packets, 1024 bytes
commands.notes: Same syntax across all four platforms (MQC — Modular QoS CLI).

commands.title: Show Class-Map (QoS)
commands.description: Displays the match criteria configured within a QoS class-map.
commands.sample_output:
Class Map match-any VOICE (id 2)
  Match  dscp ef (46)
commands.notes: Used together with 'show policy-map' to fully audit a QoS deployment.

commands.title: Show Access Lists
commands.description: Displays configured ACLs and per-entry match (hit) counters.
commands.sample_output:
Standard IP access list 10
    10 permit 10.0.0.0, wildcard bits 0.0.0.255 (2 matches)
commands.notes: NX-OS requires the address-family: 'show ip access-lists' (IPv4) or 'show ipv6 access-lists'.

commands.title: Configure an Extended ACL
commands.description: Creates a named/numbered extended ACL to permit or deny traffic based on L3/L4 criteria.
commands.ios:
ip access-list extended <name>
 permit tcp any any eq 443
commands.ios_xe:
ip access-list extended <name>
 permit tcp any any eq 443
commands.ios_xr:
ipv4 access-list <name>
 10 permit tcp any any eq 443
commands.nx_os:
ip access-list <name>
 permit tcp any any eq 443
commands.example:
ip access-list extended WEB-ONLY
 permit tcp any any eq 443
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show access-lists')
commands.notes: IOS-XR ACL entries are explicitly sequence-numbered ('10 permit ...'); classic IOS auto-assigns sequence numbers.

commands.title: Show NAT Translations
commands.description: Displays active NAT translation entries: inside local/global and outside local/global address pairs.
commands.sample_output:
Pro Inside global   Inside local     Outside local    Outside global
tcp 203.0.113.5:1024 10.0.0.5:1024  198.51.100.9:443 198.51.100.9:443
commands.notes: IOS-XR uses the NAT44-specific keyword 'show nat44 translations'.

commands.title: Show NAT Statistics
commands.description: Shows aggregate NAT counters: active translations, hits/misses, pool usage.
commands.sample_output:
Total active translations: 152 (0 static, 152 dynamic; 152 extended)
Hits: 102934  Misses: 12
commands.notes: A high 'Misses' count with an exhausted pool usually indicates the NAT pool needs to be expanded.

commands.title: Show VRF
commands.description: Lists configured VRFs (Virtual Routing and Forwarding instances) and their Route Distinguisher / interfaces.
commands.sample_output:
Name             Default RD          Protocols   Interfaces
CUSTOMER-A       65001:100           ipv4        Gi0/1.100
commands.notes: IOS-XR requires the 'all' keyword to list every VRF: 'show vrf all'.

commands.title: Configure a VRF
commands.description: Creates a VRF and assigns it a Route Distinguisher, isolating a routing/forwarding table for multi-tenant or MPLS L3VPN designs.
commands.ios:
vrf definition <name>
 rd <asn>:<id>
 address-family ipv4
 exit-address-family
commands.ios_xe:
vrf definition <name>
 rd <asn>:<id>
 address-family ipv4
 exit-address-family
commands.example:
vrf definition CUSTOMER-A
 rd 65001:100
 address-family ipv4
 exit-address-family
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show vrf')
commands.notes: NX-OS keyword is 'vrf context' rather than 'vrf definition'.

commands.title: Enable AAA New-Model
commands.description: Enables the AAA (Authentication, Authorization, Accounting) subsystem, required before any TACACS+/RADIUS configuration takes effect.
commands.ios_xr: aaa authentication login default group tacacs+ local
commands.nx_os:
feature tacacs+
aaa authentication login default group tacacs+
commands.sample_output: (no output)
commands.notes: IOS-XR does not use a separate 'aaa new-model' toggle; AAA method-lists are configured directly. NX-OS requires enabling the relevant 'feature' first.

commands.title: Configure a RADIUS Server
commands.description: Defines a RADIUS server used for authentication/authorization/accounting.
commands.ios:
radius server <name>
 address ipv4 <ip> auth-port 1812 acct-port 1813
 key <secret>
commands.ios_xe:
radius server <name>
 address ipv4 <ip> auth-port 1812 acct-port 1813
 key <secret>
commands.nx_os:
feature radius
radius-server host <ip> key <secret> authentication accounting
commands.syntax: radius server <name> / address ipv4 <ip> auth-port <port> acct-port <port> / key <secret>
commands.example:
radius server ISE-01
 address ipv4 10.0.0.50 auth-port 1812 acct-port 1813
 key MySecretKey
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show radius server-group all' or 'test aaa')
commands.notes: NX-OS requires 'feature radius' to be enabled first.

commands.title: Configure a TACACS+ Server
commands.description: Defines a TACACS+ server, typically used for device administration AAA (command authorization/accounting).
commands.example:
tacacs server TACACS-01
 address ipv4 10.0.0.60
 key MySecretKey
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show tacacs')
commands.notes: NX-OS requires 'feature tacacs+' first.

commands.title: Show HSRP Status
commands.description: Displays HSRP (Hot Standby Router Protocol) group state: Active/Standby role, priority, virtual IP.
commands.sample_output:
Interface  Grp  Pri P State   Active          Standby         Virtual IP
Gi0/1      10   110  Active  local           10.0.0.2        10.0.0.254
commands.notes: IOS-XR uses the protocol name directly: 'show hsrp' (no 'standby' keyword, since 'standby' is IOS/IOS-XE specific CLI naming).

commands.title: Show VRRP Status
commands.description: Displays VRRP (Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol) group state — the open-standard equivalent of HSRP.
commands.sample_output:
Interface  Grp  Pri  Time  Own Pre State    Master addr     Group addr
Gi0/1      10   100  3609  Y   Y   Master   10.0.0.1        10.0.0.254
commands.notes: Same syntax family across platforms; VRRP is standards-based (RFC 5798) so behaviour is consistent.

commands.title: Show GLBP Status
commands.description: Displays GLBP (Gateway Load Balancing Protocol) group and AVF/AVG state, allowing active-active first-hop load balancing.
commands.sample_output:
Interface  Grp  Fwd Pri State    Address         Active router   Standby router
Gi0/1      10   -   100 Active   10.0.0.254      local           10.0.0.2
commands.notes: GLBP is a Cisco-proprietary protocol; it is not available on IOS-XR.

commands.title: Show PIM Neighbors
commands.description: Displays PIM (Protocol Independent Multicast) neighbor adjacency and DR (Designated Router) role per interface.
commands.sample_output:
Neighbor Address  Interface        Uptime/Expires   Ver  DR Prio/Mode
10.0.0.2          GigabitEthernet0/1  3d02h/00:01:30  v2   1 / DR S P G
commands.notes: Same core syntax minus the 'ip' keyword on IOS-XR.

commands.title: Show IP Multicast Routing Table
commands.description: Displays the multicast routing table: (S,G) and (*,G) entries, incoming/outgoing interface lists.
commands.sample_output:
(*, 239.1.1.1), 00:10:23/stopped, RP 10.0.0.9, flags: S
  Incoming interface: Gi0/1
  Outgoing interface list: Gi0/2, Forward/Sparse
commands.notes: IOS-XR uses the MRIB (Multicast RIB) view: 'show mrib route'.

commands.title: Show IGMP Groups
commands.description: Displays multicast group membership learned via IGMP on each interface.
commands.sample_output:
IGMP Connected Group Membership
Group Address    Interface   Uptime    Expires   Last Reporter
239.1.1.1        Gi0/1       00:10:00  00:02:30  10.0.0.5
commands.notes: Consistent with the PIM naming convention (drop 'ip' on XR).

commands.title: Configure SNMP Community
commands.description: Configures an SNMPv2c community string for read-only (RO) or read-write (RW) polling access.
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show snmp community' or 'show running-config | include snmp')
commands.notes: Prefer SNMPv3 with authentication/encryption for production; plain community strings are sent in clear text.

commands.title: Show SNMP Status
commands.description: Displays SNMP engine statistics: packets in/out, errors, and whether the agent is enabled.
commands.sample_output:
Chassis: 12345678
123742 SNMP packets input
commands.notes: Identical across all four platforms.

commands.title: Configure NetFlow / Flexible NetFlow
commands.description: Configures NetFlow/Flexible NetFlow export of traffic flow records to a collector for visibility and capacity planning.
commands.ios:
ip flow-export destination <ip> <port>
interface <name>
 ip flow ingress
commands.nx_os:
feature netflow
flow exporter <name>
 destination <ip> use-vrf default
commands.example:
flow exporter NF-COLLECTOR
 destination 10.0.0.100
 transport udp 2055
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show flow exporter' / 'show flow monitor')
commands.notes: Classic IOS uses the legacy 'ip flow-export' syntax; IOS-XE/XR/NX-OS use the newer Flexible NetFlow exporter-map model.

commands.title: Show NetFlow Statistics
commands.description: Displays exported flow statistics and cache utilization for NetFlow monitors.
commands.sample_output:
Cache type:                             Normal
Cache size:                             4096
Current entries:                        152
commands.notes: Legacy classic IOS uses the older 'show ip cache flow' command from original NetFlow (not Flexible NetFlow).

commands.title: Configure Model-Driven Telemetry
commands.description: Configures streaming (model-driven) telemetry, pushing YANG-modeled operational data to a collector in near real-time — a modern alternative to SNMP polling.
commands.ios_xe:
telemetry ietf subscription <id>
 encoding encode-kvgpb
 receiver ip address <ip> <port>
commands.nx_os:
feature telemetry
telemetry
 destination-group <name>
  ip address <ip> port <port>
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show telemetry model-driven subscription')
commands.notes: Not available on legacy classic IOS; requires an XE/XR/NX-OS train with YANG/gRPC or gNMI support.

commands.title: Configure Syslog Logging Host
commands.description: Configures a remote syslog server that will receive log messages generated by the device.
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show logging')
commands.notes: IOS-XR drops the 'host' keyword ('logging <ip>'); NX-OS uses 'logging server <ip>'.

commands.title: Show Logging Buffer
commands.description: Displays the local logging buffer contents — the internal history of syslog messages generated by the device.
commands.sample_output: 000123: *Jul 19 08:00:00.123: %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/1, changed state to up
commands.notes: NX-OS distinguishes 'show logging log' (the buffer) from 'show logging' (config summary).

commands.title: Debug IP Packet
commands.description: Enables real-time debugging of IP packets process-switched by the router, optionally filtered by an ACL.
commands.notes: Extremely CPU-intensive on production routers — always scope with an ACL and disable immediately after ('undebug all') once done.

commands.title: Debug BGP Events
commands.description: Enables debugging of BGP finite-state-machine events — session establishment, teardown, and error notifications.
commands.sample_output: BGP: 192.0.2.1 went from Idle to Active
commands.notes: On a router with many peers this can generate a large volume of output; consider 'debug ip bgp <neighbor> events' to scope it, where supported.

commands.title: Configure Port Security
commands.description: Restricts the number/identity of MAC addresses allowed on a switchport, mitigating MAC-flooding and rogue-device attacks.
commands.ios:
interface <name>
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 1
commands.ios_xe:
interface <name>
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 1
commands.nx_os:
interface <name>
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 1
commands.syntax: interface <name> / switchport port-security / switchport port-security maximum <n>
commands.example:
interface GigabitEthernet1/0/1
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 1
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show port-security interface <name>')
commands.notes: Port-security is an L2 switching feature; not applicable on IOS-XR (used on pure routing platforms).

commands.title: Configure DHCP Snooping
commands.description: Enables DHCP Snooping, a Layer 2 security feature that filters untrusted DHCP messages and builds a binding table to prevent rogue DHCP servers.
commands.ios:
ip dhcp snooping
ip dhcp snooping vlan <vlan-id>
commands.ios_xe:
ip dhcp snooping
ip dhcp snooping vlan <vlan-id>
commands.nx_os:
feature dhcp
ip dhcp snooping
ip dhcp snooping vlan <vlan-id>
commands.syntax: ip dhcp snooping / ip dhcp snooping vlan <vlan-id>
commands.example:
ip dhcp snooping
ip dhcp snooping vlan 10
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show ip dhcp snooping')
commands.notes: NX-OS requires 'feature dhcp' before DHCP snooping commands become available.

commands.title: Show CPU Utilization
commands.description: Displays per-process CPU utilization — the first command to run when a device becomes sluggish or drops packets under load.
commands.sample_output:
CPU utilization for five seconds: 23%/5%; one minute: 20%; five minutes: 18%
 PID Runtime(ms) Invoked  uSecs  5Sec  1Min  5Min TTY Process
commands.notes: NX-OS spells the sort keyword without the trailing 'ed': 'sort' instead of 'sorted'.

commands.title: Show Memory Utilization
commands.description: Displays per-process memory allocation — useful for diagnosing memory leaks or exhaustion.
commands.sample_output:
Total: 4294967296, Used: 1073741824, Free: 3221225472
 PID  TTY  Allocated  Freed  Holding  Getbufs  Retbufs  Process
commands.notes: Compare 'Holding' across polls over time to spot a leaking process.

commands.title: Show MAC Address Table
commands.description: Displays the L2 forwarding table mapping learned MAC addresses to VLAN and switchport.
commands.sample_output:
Vlan    Mac Address       Type        Ports
10      0011.2233.44aa    DYNAMIC     Gi1/0/1
commands.notes: IOS-XR routers are not L2 switches by default — the closest equivalent is the L2VPN forwarding MAC table, only relevant if L2VPN/bridging is configured.

commands.title: Show VLAN Brief
commands.description: Lists all configured VLANs, their state, and assigned ports.
commands.sample_output:
VLAN Name          Status    Ports
10   USERS         active    Gi1/0/1, Gi1/0/2
commands.notes: Not applicable to IOS-XR (pure L3 routing platform, no native VLAN database).

commands.title: Create a VLAN
commands.description: Creates a VLAN and assigns it a descriptive name in the VLAN database.
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show vlan brief')
commands.notes: Not applicable on IOS-XR.

commands.title: Show Spanning-Tree Status
commands.description: Displays Spanning Tree Protocol topology: root bridge election, port roles (Root/Designated/Blocking) and states.
commands.sample_output:
VLAN0010
  Spanning tree enabled protocol rstp
  Root ID    Priority    32778
commands.notes: Not applicable on IOS-XR routers, which don't run classic STP.

commands.title: Enable Rapid-PVST (RSTP)
commands.description: Switches the spanning-tree algorithm from legacy 802.1D STP to Rapid-PVST+ (802.1w per-VLAN), which converges in seconds instead of ~50s.
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show spanning-tree summary')
commands.notes: Rapid-PVST+ is Cisco's per-VLAN implementation of 802.1w.

commands.title: Configure MST (Multiple Spanning Tree)
commands.description: Configures Multiple Spanning Tree (802.1s), mapping VLAN ranges to STP instances to reduce the number of STP topologies computed.
commands.ios:
spanning-tree mst configuration
 name <region-name>
 revision <n>
 instance 1 vlan 1-100
commands.ios_xe:
spanning-tree mst configuration
 name <region-name>
 revision <n>
 instance 1 vlan 1-100
commands.nx_os:
spanning-tree mst configuration
 name <region-name>
 revision <n>
 instance 1 vlan 1-100
commands.syntax: spanning-tree mst configuration / name <region> / revision <n> / instance <id> vlan <range>
commands.example:
spanning-tree mst configuration
 name REGION1
 revision 1
 instance 1 vlan 1-100
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show spanning-tree mst configuration')
commands.notes: All switches in the same MST region must have an identical name/revision/VLAN-to-instance mapping or they form separate regions.

commands.title: Show EtherChannel Summary
commands.description: Displays EtherChannel/port-channel bundle status and member-port state (Bndl/Susp/Down/etc).
commands.sample_output:
Group  Port-channel  Protocol   Ports
1      Po1(SU)       LACP       Gi0/1(P) Gi0/2(P)
commands.notes: NX-OS uses 'show port-channel summary'; IOS-XR uses 'show bundle' (its bundle-interface abstraction).

commands.title: Configure an EtherChannel (LACP)
commands.description: Bundles multiple physical links into a single logical LACP EtherChannel for redundancy and increased bandwidth.
commands.ios:
interface range Gi0/1 - 2
 channel-group 1 mode active
commands.ios_xe:
interface range Gi0/1 - 2
 channel-group 1 mode active
commands.ios_xr:
interface Bundle-Ether1
interface Gi0/0/0/1
 bundle id 1 mode active
commands.example:
interface range GigabitEthernet0/1 - 2
 channel-group 1 mode active
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show etherchannel summary')
commands.notes: IOS-XR uses the 'Bundle-Ether' interface abstraction and 'bundle id' under each member interface rather than 'channel-group'.

commands.title: Show LACP Neighbor
commands.description: Displays LACP partner (neighbor) system ID, port priority and state flags for each bundled link.
commands.sample_output:
Flags:  S - Device is requesting Slow LACPDUs
Channel group 1 neighbors
  Port     Flags   State   LACP port Priority
commands.notes: IOS-XR's bundle CLI consolidates this info under 'show lacp'.

commands.title: Show UDLD Status
commands.description: Displays UDLD (Unidirectional Link Detection) status — detects and disables links where traffic flows in only one direction (e.g. broken fiber pair).
commands.sample_output:
Interface Gi0/1
---
Port enable administrative configuration setting: Enabled
Current bidirectional state: Bidirectional
commands.notes: UDLD is a Cisco proprietary L2 feature typically used on switching platforms, not on IOS-XR routers.

commands.title: Show CDP Neighbors
commands.description: Displays directly connected Cisco devices discovered via CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) — device ID, platform, and port.
commands.sample_output:
Device ID: SW2
  Platform: cisco WS-C3850-24T, Capabilities: Switch IGMP
  Interface: GigabitEthernet0/1, Port ID: GigabitEthernet1/0/1
commands.notes: Consistent syntax across all four platforms.

commands.title: Show LLDP Neighbors
commands.description: Displays directly connected devices discovered via LLDP (vendor-neutral equivalent of CDP), useful in multi-vendor environments.
commands.sample_output:
Chassis id: 0011.2233.4499
Port id: Gi1/0/1
System Name: SW2
commands.notes: Prefer LLDP over CDP when peering with non-Cisco equipment.

commands.title: Show DHCP Bindings
commands.description: Displays active DHCP lease bindings when the router/switch is acting as a DHCP server.
commands.sample_output:
IP address       Client-ID/         Lease expiration        Type
10.0.0.50         0100.1122.3344      Jul 20 2026 08:00 AM  Automatic
commands.notes: IOS-XR requires the address-family: 'show dhcp ipv4 binding'.

commands.title: Configure a DHCP Pool
commands.description: Configures the router as a local DHCP server for a subnet, defining the address pool, default gateway and DNS servers to hand out.
commands.ios:
ip dhcp pool <name>
 network <network> <mask>
 default-router <gw>
 dns-server <dns>
commands.ios_xe:
ip dhcp pool <name>
 network <network> <mask>
 default-router <gw>
 dns-server <dns>
commands.ios_xr:
dhcp ipv4
 pool <name>
  network <network>/<len>
  dns-server <dns>
  default-router <gw>
commands.nx_os:
feature dhcp
ip dhcp pool <name>
 network <network> <mask>
commands.example:
ip dhcp pool USERS
 network 10.0.0.0 255.255.255.0
 default-router 10.0.0.1
 dns-server 8.8.8.8
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show ip dhcp binding')
commands.notes: NX-OS requires 'feature dhcp' first; IOS-XR nests the pool inside a 'dhcp ipv4' top-level block.

commands.title: Configure DNS Resolution
commands.description: Configures upstream DNS servers the device itself uses for hostname resolution (e.g. for ping/traceroute by name).
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show hosts' or 'show running-config | include name-server')
commands.notes: IOS-XR spells the enable keyword 'domain lookup' (no 'ip' prefix).

commands.title: Show DNS Host Cache
commands.description: Displays the local hostname-to-IP cache, including static host entries and cached DNS lookups.
commands.sample_output:
Host              Flags      Age Type   Address(es)
router2.local     (perm, OK) 0   IP     10.0.0.2
commands.notes: Consistent syntax on all four platforms.

commands.title: Configure NTP
commands.description: Configures an upstream NTP time source to synchronize the device clock — critical for accurate logging/troubleshooting correlation.
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show ntp status' / 'show ntp associations')
commands.notes: NX-OS requires an explicit VRF context for NTP: 'use-vrf default' (or a named VRF) if not using the management VRF.

commands.title: Show NTP Status
commands.description: Displays whether the local clock is synchronized, the reference clock, stratum and offset.
commands.sample_output:
Clock is synchronized, stratum 3, reference is 132.163.97.1
nominal freq is 250.0000 Hz, actual freq is 249.9997 Hz
commands.notes: NX-OS uses 'show ntp peer-status' rather than 'show ntp status'.

commands.title: Configure IP SLA
commands.description: Configures IP SLA to continuously measure reachability/latency/jitter to a target, often paired with tracking objects for failover.
commands.ios:
ip sla 1
 icmp-echo <dest-ip>
 frequency 30
ip sla schedule 1 life forever start-time now
commands.ios_xe:
ip sla 1
 icmp-echo <dest-ip>
 frequency 30
ip sla schedule 1 life forever start-time now
commands.ios_xr:
ipsla
 operation 1
  type icmp echo
   destination address <dest-ip>
commands.nx_os:
feature sla sender
ip sla 1
 icmp-echo <dest-ip>
 frequency 30
commands.example:
ip sla 1
 icmp-echo 10.0.0.9
 frequency 30
ip sla schedule 1 life forever start-time now
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show ip sla statistics')
commands.notes: NX-OS requires 'feature sla sender'; IOS-XR uses a distinctly different 'ipsla' operation syntax.

commands.title: Show IP SLA Statistics
commands.description: Displays the latest round-trip-time/reachability results recorded by an IP SLA operation.
commands.sample_output:
Round Trip Time (RTT) for Index 1
        Latest RTT: 2 ms
Return Code: OK
commands.notes: IOS-XR spells the command without a space: 'show ipsla statistics'.

commands.title: Configure Policy-Based Routing (PBR)
commands.description: Forces traffic matching specific criteria to take a different next-hop/path than the routing table would normally select.
commands.ios:
route-map PBR permit 10
 match ip address 101
 set ip next-hop <ip>
interface <name>
 ip policy route-map PBR
commands.ios_xe:
route-map PBR permit 10
 match ip address 101
 set ip next-hop <ip>
interface <name>
 ip policy route-map PBR
commands.ios_xr:
route-policy PBR
 if destination in (10.0.0.0/24) then
  set next-hop <ip>
 endif
interface <name>
 ipv4 policy route-map PBR
commands.nx_os:
route-map PBR permit 10
 match ip address 101
 set ip next-hop <ip>
interface <name>
 ip policy route-map PBR
commands.syntax: route-map <name> permit <seq> / match ip address <acl> / set ip next-hop <ip> then apply under interface with 'ip policy route-map'
commands.example:
route-map PBR permit 10
 match ip address 101
 set ip next-hop 10.0.0.9
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
 ip policy route-map PBR
commands.sample_output: (no output; verify with 'show route-map' and 'show ip policy')
commands.notes: IOS-XR uses RPL (route-policy) with an if/then/endif syntax rather than classic route-maps.

commands.title: Show IP Policy (PBR) Bindings
commands.description: Lists which interfaces have a PBR route-map applied.
commands.sample_output:
Interface      Route map
Gi0/1          PBR
commands.notes: IOS-XR equivalent is 'show route-policy' which lists RPL policy definitions rather than interface bindings directly.

commands.title: Show PIM RP Mapping
commands.description: Displays the Rendezvous Point (RP) to multicast group mapping, learned via static config, Auto-RP, or BSR.
commands.sample_output:
PIM Group-to-RP Mappings
Group(s) 224.0.0.0/4
  RP 10.0.0.9 (?), v2
    Info source: Static
commands.notes: Critical command when multicast traffic isn't forwarding — a missing/incorrect RP mapping is the #1 cause.

commands.title: Show IGMP Snooping
commands.description: Displays IGMP snooping state per VLAN — an L2 optimization that prunes multicast flooding to only ports with interested receivers.
commands.sample_output:
Vlan  IGMP snooping  Querier    v1 v2 v3   Report suppression  Immediate Leave
10    Enabled        Non-Querier ---- Yes ----   Enabled              Disabled
commands.notes: This is an L2 switching feature; not present on IOS-XR routers.

commands.title: Show Version
commands.description: Displays hardware model, software version/train, uptime, and installed feature licenses — the standard first command on any device.
commands.sample_output:
Cisco IOS XE Software, Version 17.09.04a
ROM: IOS-XE ROMMON
Uptime is 45 days, 3 hours, 12 minutes
commands.notes: Consistent core syntax across all platforms, though output formatting differs significantly per OS family.

commands.title: Show Running Configuration
commands.description: Displays the active configuration currently running in memory (as opposed to the saved startup-config).
commands.sample_output:
Building configuration...
!
hostname ROUTER1
!
interface GigabitEthernet0/1
commands.notes: On IOS-XR, running-config is generated from the committed configuration database, not edited directly in real time.

commands.title: Save Running Configuration
commands.description: Persists the running configuration to non-volatile memory so it survives a reload.
commands.sample_output:
Building configuration...
[OK]
commands.notes: IOS-XR uses a transactional model: configuration changes must be explicitly 'commit'ted, and are persistent once committed (no separate save step needed).
commands.configuration_mode: No (privileged EXEC action command; IOS-XR: config mode 'commit')

commands.title: Reload the Device
commands.description: Reboots the device, optionally scheduled for a later time — commonly used with a safety timer during risky changes.
commands.sample_output:
System configuration has been modified. Save? [yes/no]:
Proceed with reload? [confirm]
commands.notes: Always schedule a 'reload in <n>' safety-net before making risky remote changes, and cancel it with 'reload cancel' once you confirm connectivity.

commands.title: Ping
commands.description: Sends ICMP echo requests to test basic IP reachability and measure round-trip time.
commands.sample_output:
Sending 5, 100-byte ICMP Echos to 8.8.8.8, timeout is 2 seconds:
!!!!!
Success rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 1/2/4 ms
commands.notes: Extended ping (typing 'ping' with no arguments) lets you set source interface, VRF, packet size and repeat count interactively.

commands.title: Traceroute
commands.description: Maps the hop-by-hop path packets take to a destination using incrementing TTL and ICMP time-exceeded replies.
commands.sample_output:
Tracing the route to 8.8.8.8
  1 192.0.2.1 1 msec 0 msec 1 msec
  2 198.51.100.1 5 msec 4 msec 5 msec
commands.notes: A hop showing '* * *' can indicate an ICMP rate-limit/filter rather than an actual outage — verify with other tools before concluding a link is down.

commands.title: Show Processes
commands.description: Lists all running processes on the device with their state, CPU and runtime — a broader view than 'show processes cpu'.
commands.sample_output:
PID QTy  PC  State   Runtime(ms)  Invoked  uSecs  Stacks TTY Process
1   Lwe 1010203C Waiting  0  1  0  6000/6000 0  Chunk Manager
commands.notes: Useful for confirming whether a specific protocol process (e.g. 'BGP Router', 'OSPF Router') is running at all.

commands.title: Show Hardware Inventory
commands.description: Lists all hardware components (chassis, line cards, PSUs, SFPs) with PIDs, serial numbers and descriptions — commonly needed for RMA/warranty requests.
commands.sample_output:
NAME: "Chassis", DESCR: "Cisco ASR1001-X Chassis"
PID: ASR1001-X, VID: V07, SN: FXS1234A1BC
commands.notes: Always include the full 'show inventory' output when opening a Cisco TAC hardware case.

commands.title: Show Environment (Power/Temp/Fan)
commands.description: Displays chassis environmental sensors: power supply status, fan status, and temperature readings.
commands.sample_output:
SYSTEM TEMPERATURES
Sensor      Location  Reading(°C) Status
Inlet       Chassis   28          OK
commands.notes: A common first check for random reloads that turn out to be thermal shutdowns or a failed power supply.
